title: Caladan: Mitigating Interference at Microsecond Timescales
abstract: Caladan is a per-core scheduler that detects interference between co-located workloads via control signals (queueing delay, cache misses, memory bandwidth) and reallocates cores at microsecond granularity. It outperforms Shenango on mixed latency-throughput workloads by 1.4-2.1x, but its control-loop tuning assumes a single-tenant cluster and degrades under multi-tenant churn.

title: Anna: A KVS for Any Scale
abstract: Anna achieves coordination-free linearizability through monotonic CRDTs and a wait-free per-thread datastore. Its multi-master design scales near-linearly to 100s of cores on a single machine and gracefully across geo-distributed deployments. The CRDT model, however, restricts the supported value types to those forming valid lattices, limiting expressiveness vs RocksDB.

title: io_uring: Asynchronous I/O for Linux at Scale
abstract: io_uring exposes a shared-memory ring-buffer interface between userspace and the kernel, eliminating syscall overhead for I/O submission. End-to-end throughput gains of 3-7x are reported for storage workloads, but the async semantics complicate crash consistency and require careful programming to avoid TOCTOU races on the ring.
